notes: This game is neither "Native" nor usable via Proton. You may start the game and enjoy very minimal usage of single player maps but you CANNOT PLAY ONLINE. Do some quick Googling for further reading but this game is very incompatible with Linux. After making many, many adjustments trying to access popular server - neither I nor many have been successful.
title: Garry's Mod

notes: Almost entirely flawless. Minor issues such as Backspace not working with certain LUA text inputs, this does not affect the Console or Chat systems. The issue with some servers crashing upon connection is dependant on the servers themselves, and not of platforms. Most work fine unless they are peppered with addons that are incompatible with each-other.
title: Garry's Mod

notes: Generally crashes with lots of installed addons, please dump this port, it is broken.
title: Garry's Mod

notes: It's a 32-bit game which easily hits the 4GB virtual RAM limit both when running with native or proton. Actual RAM usage is pretty low though (always less than 3GB). Joining any game server with more than ~20 players crashes the game. Single player is reliable on both native and proton. Lowering your resolution to 720p and setting textures and models to low lowers the RAM usage and with some luck you are then able to join servers with 30-40 players online. If a server has more mods it uses more RAM so stay away from highly customized servers to avoid crashing. Proton version also seems to use less virtual RAM.
title: Garry's Mod

notes: This port has major issues running with any sort of addons at all. Creating a singleplayer game with more than 50 addons crashes it. Singleplayer without any addons run without issue, however.
title: Garry's Mod

notes: Linux version is generally unplayable, get the Steam Proton version.. much better. can also multiplayer with 30+ players
title: Garry's Mod

cpu: AMD Ryzen 3 2200G with Radeon Vega Graphics
notes: Has similar performance to the Windows version, and runs stable with 100+ addons. Chromium branch doesn't seem to display HUD elements, however. Much better than the trash Linux port they have out now.
title: Garry's Mod

notes: Works with native, just make sure you go to BETAs under game preferences and choose the x86_64 experimental build to play on servers with a lot of mods, or else you will get LuaJIT out of memory errors.
title: Garry's Mod

notes: Game works native on Linux. On Proton the game works too out of the box. I installed manually D9VK and it worked without graphical glitches too with this DX9 to Vulkan translation!
title: Garry's Mod

notes: Works like a charm after install d9vk. I installed it easily via protontricks. No glitches, stable fps.
title: Garry's Mod

notes: Runs as expected with <50 or so mods anything more and the game will not load past the title screen
title: Garry's Mod

notes: This will play SP/Lightly modded Sanbox MP natively. It seems to be 32 bit however, meaning if you go on any server with a decent amount of mods you're going to be hounded with game engine errors and lua panics. I found no solution to this issue, should not be happening when "native".
title: Garry's Mod

notes:
It works perfect, even on super bloated darkrp server. I use x86_64 branch and
GloriousEggroll's proton build (which is includes chromium embedded framework patches from wine staging). D9VK default enabled on this custom proton build.
title: Garry's Mod

notes: Everything works out of box. No problems with D9VK. Performance is similar to the native version.
title: Garry's Mod
